{{Quote|Tell the fleet to begin the jump to lightspeed! I want that conference site turned into a crater! I plan to wipe several layers of scum from beneath my boots!|[[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1998) 36">[[Star Wars (1998) 36|''Star Wars'' (1998) 36]]</ref>}}
The '''First Battle of Qotile''' was the opening battle of the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]].

==The prologue==
[[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]] had been chosen to be the host of a meeting between the [[Stark Commercial Combine]], [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] to debate the Combine's seizure of [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] shipments for needy worlds during an apparent bacta shortage. However, [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]] had assembled a [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|secret fleet]] with the goal of disrupting the talks.

The Jedi and Republic negotiators arrived on Troiken, meeting with Stark and his Combine co-directors. Once the inaugural meeting began, Federation [[Trade Federation Minister|Minister]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] activated a homing beacon on his [[Mechno-chair/Legends|mechno-chair]], transmitting the coordinates of the meeting to Eriaduan's fleet. Tarkin ordered the ships to enter [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], intending to attack the summit and kill both the Combine's leadership and the Republic's entourage.

No sooner had the Combine's leadership been seated than [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tyvokka/Legends|Tyvokka]], the lead [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] negotiator present, suggested that they skip the formalities and get to what Combine leader [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark]] intended to be the real solution. Stark agreed, and his men drew their weapons on the Republic negotiators, using the pretext of Gunray's treachery.

==The battle==
Stark revealed his knowledge of the Eriaduan's fleet—as well as the fact that he had piggybacked his Combine's [[navcomputer virus]] on Gunray's signal, incapacitating the navigational computers of Tarkin's ships.
[[File:Jinn_Gunray_Troiken.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Qui-Gon Jinn and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] protecting [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]]]]
Many of Tarkin's vessels emerged from hyperspace in areas that would destroy them (such as near stars or black holes), others emerged in realspace safely but far off course, and only a fraction—including Tarkin's flagship [[Invincible (Stark Hyperspace War)|''Invincible'']]—made it to the [[Troiken system/Legends|Troiken system]], where they immediately came under fire from the Combine ships that outnumbered them.

Although Republic negotiator [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] protested that he knew nothing of Tarkin's unsanctioned plans, Stark would hear none of it, insisting that the Republic team surrender. Tyvokka, realizing that his earlier suspicions about Stark's duplicity were true, literally turned the tables on Stark's men, with the Jedi springing into action.

Under [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn's]] advice, the panicked Gunray ordered his [[HKB-3 hunter-killer droid|security droids]] to "kill them all." But the droids opened fire on both Stark's men ''and'' the Jedi, mortally wounding Tyvokka and forcing the Jedi to destroy the droids while Stark and his co-directors escaped.

==Regrouping==
The Jedi and their charges quickly made their way back to their [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular'' space cruiser]] while they still could (Gunray's [[Trade Federation shuttle|transport]] having been destroyed just before their escape), retreating to [[Mount Avos]].

[[File:Tarkin_Paramilitary_Troops.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Troops of Tarkin's paramilitary forces are attacked on the surface of [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]].]]

Tyvokka died en route, ordering [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] to protect Valorum to ensure a peaceful solution, [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] to rally Tarkin's troops lest the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] surrender (as well as to join the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] as Tyvokka had hoped), and Jinn to seek a way to override the computer virus. En route, Koon used his telepathic abilities to scare off the Stark fighter following them. Upon arrival at Avos, their damaged Consular exploded.

Overhead, less than a fourth of Tarkin's fleet remained. After the ''Invincible'' suffered critical damage and Tarkin received a message from Valorum informing him of their retreat location, Tarkin ordered his crews to abandon their ships and aim their [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] for Mount Avos.

The fighting ships of Tarkin's fleet surrounded the troop carriers while their crews left, before being evacuated themselves. The ''Invincible'' was destroyed just after Tarkin and his staff evacuated, the burning hulk impacting near Avos, as the Jedi and soldiers began to regroup inside the mountain's abandoned [[Spice/Legends|spice]] mines.
